input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML**
```xml
<RefreshView IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
             Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}">
    <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                    SelectionMode="Single"
                    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                    SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding SelectionChangedCommand}">
        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:Item">
                <Grid Padding="12" ColumnDefinitions="*,Auto">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                    <Label Grid.Column="1" Text="{Binding Status}" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel bindings you need:**

| Property/Command | Type | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `Items` | `ObservableCollection<Item>` | Data source |
| `SelectedItem` | `Item?` | Two-way bound; set back to `null` after navigation to allow reselection |
| `IsRefreshing` | `bool` | Controls the refresh spinner; set `false` when done |
| `RefreshCommand` | `IAsyncRelayCommand` | Calls your API, repopulates `Items`, then sets `IsRefreshing = false` |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` | `IAsyncRelayCommand` | Navigates to detail page, then clears `SelectedItem` |

**ViewModel sketch (CommunityToolkit.Mvvm)**
```csharp
public partial class ListViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigationService _nav;

    [ObservableProperty] ObservableCollection<Item> items = [];
    [ObservableProperty] Item? selectedItem;
    [ObservableProperty] bool isRefreshing;

    [RelayCommand]
    async Task RefreshAsync()
    {
        var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
        Items = new ObservableCollection<Item>(data);
        IsRefreshing = false;           // stops the spinner
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    async Task SelectionChangedAsync()
    {
        if (SelectedItem is null) return;
        await _nav.GoToAsync("DetailPage", new() { ["Item"] = SelectedItem });
        SelectedItem = null;            // allows re-selection of same item
    }
}
```

**Key points:**
- **`SelectionMode="Single"`** – only one item selectable at a time.
- **Reset `SelectedItem` to `null`** after navigating, otherwise tapping the same row again won't fire the command.
- **Set `IsRefreshing = false`** at the end of your refresh logic — `RefreshView` won't stop the spinner on its own.
- Wrap `CollectionView` inside `RefreshView`, not the other way around.
